sections.paragraph: [pl. כְּבָשִׂים] m. a lamb (pr. progeny of sheep), specially from the first to the third year (see Bochart, Hieroz. i. p. 421, seq.), whence there is often added בֶּן־שְׁנָתֹו the son of its year, one year old. Num 7:15, 21 , 33 , 39 , 45 , 51 , 57 , 63 , 69 , 75 , and in plur. בְּנֵי שָׁנָה Num 7:17, 23 , 29 , 35 , 41 . Sometimes it is used in a wider sense, and denotes sheep generally, Gen 21:27. [This is quite a mistake, this word does not occur there.] (كَبْشُ a lamb of a year old, see the Arabian grammarians in Bochart, loc. cit.). The feminine of this word is-
